key_insight: In multiferroic altermagnet Fe2Mo3O8, ferroelectric polarization switching and mechanical strain jointly control spin splitting, shift current sign, and magneto-optical response.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a route to strain- and polarization-tunable spintronic and optoelectronic devices with switchable nonlinear and Kerr signals.
method: Analyzed coupling among polarization, spin-splitting texture, shift current, and magneto-optical spectra
Evaluated the effect of ferroelectric switching and uniaxial a-axis strain
evidence_signal: The abstract reports systematic computational results showing polarization reversal inverts shift-current sign, strain tunes spectra, and a-axis strain activates a finite Kerr effect.
watch_item: The reported effects are computational and may depend sensitively on ideal crystal symmetry, strain control, and experimental sample quality.
paper_title: Strain-Tunable Shift Current and Magneto-Optical Kerr Effect in Multiferroic Altermagnet Fe2Mo3O8
